title: leontodon hastilis subsp. hispidus
intro: Leontodon hastilis subsp. hispidus, also known as the hairy hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and menstrual problems due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The hairy hawk's beard is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating menstrual cramps, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. It is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, with the leaves and flowers being the most commonly used parts of the plant.
